task_completion.visual_evidence: IntelliJ IDEA or another IDE is open, showing the contents of 'src/main/java/com/example/Main.java'.

task_completion.description: Set a conditional breakpoint for i == 888.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A breakpoint properties dialog/popup is visible showing the condition 'i == 888', OR the debugger is later shown paused exactly at i=888 without any modifications to the source code.

task_completion.description: Pause the debugger at the 888th iteration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The IDE's debugger pane is active, execution is paused (indicated by a highlighted line in the editor), and the variables view clearly shows the variable 'i' with the value 888.
In the debugger's variables view, the variable 'currentProcessValue' is visible along with its calculated numeric value.
A terminal window shows a command (e.g., echo '<value>' > /home/ga/solution.txt) or a text editor is open saving the numeric value to '/home/ga/solution.txt'.

integrity.description: The source code of Main.java must remain unmodified.
integrity.visual_evidence: The code in Main.java does not contain any added print statements, hardcoded checks (e.g., if (i == 888)), or other modifications. The file tab should not show an unsaved changes indicator.

integrity.description: The agent used the debugger to find the value.
integrity.visual_evidence: The value is seen in the debugger's variables pane, rather than being printed to the console output via modified code or a separate script.

integrity.description: The solution is saved to the correct file path.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file path '/home/ga/solution.txt' is explicitly visible in the terminal command or text editor used to save the value.
